The Great Gatsby

The Great Gatsby

by F. Scott Fitzgerald
★★★★☆
3.93 5,959,065 ratings · 97,342 reviews

The Great Gatsby, F. Scott Fitzgerald's third book, stands as the supreme achievement of his career. This exemplary novel of the Jazz Age has been acclaimed by generations of readers.

The story of the fabulously wealthy Jay Gatsby and his love for the beautiful Daisy Buchanan, of lavish parties on Long Island at a time when The New York Times noted "ichever combats the forces of lawlessness," is an exquisitely crafted tale of America in the 1920s.

Genres
Classics Fiction Historical Fiction Literature School Novels American

180 pages, Paperback

First published April 10, 1925
